description: fraction of days with daytime high 50-85°F
source: PRISM daily normals 1991-2020 (tmax, 4km)
PRISM daily normals 1991-2020 (tmax, 4km)
PRISM daily normals 1991-2020 (tmax, 4km)

description: Daytime-high comfort. comfort_index = comfortable_days - 2*hot_days - cold_days (heat penalised 2x cold), then percentile-ranked across all towns so both heat and cold extremes land low and the distribution is well spread.
source: PRISM daily normals 1991-2020 (tmax, 4km)
normalization_method: percentile rank of weighted comfort index (comfortable-2*hot-cold)

source: Global Solar Atlas annual GHI (Solargis/World Bank)

description: Sunlight. Higher annual GHI scores higher.
source: Global Solar Atlas annual GHI (Solargis/World Bank)
normalization_method: percentile rank (higher raw = better); NaN imputed at 50

source: PRISM annual precipitation (ppt) + annual mean RH (tdmean + tmean), 4km normals
PRISM annual precipitation (ppt) + annual mean RH (tdmean + tmean), 4km normals
PRISM annual precipitation (ppt) + annual mean RH (tdmean + tmean), 4km normals

description: Mould-growth propensity. Wetness composite = 0.5*percentile(annual precipitation) + 0.5*percentile(annual mean relative humidity); drier (less rain + lower humidity) scores higher. Annual figures are used so year-round damp climates (e.g. the Pacific NW, wet/cool most of the year rather than summer-muggy) are captured. Annual mean dewpoint is also retained as a raw column.
source: PRISM annual precipitation (ppt) + annual mean RH (tdmean + tmean), 4km normals
ERA5 hourly MSLP, 2021-2023 (Copernicus CDS)

description: Daily pressure swing: mean over days of (daily max - daily min MSLP). Smaller swing scores higher. Directly measured from ERA5.
source: ERA5 hourly MSLP, 2021-2023 (Copernicus CDS)
normalization_method: percentile rank (lower raw = better); NaN imputed at 50

source: ERA5 hourly MSLP, 2021-2023 (Copernicus CDS)

description: Synoptic (day-to-day) pressure variability: standard deviation of daily-mean MSLP across all days. Lower variability scores higher. Captures frontal frequency directly (e.g. flags the stormy Pacific NW), which the former continentality proxy could not.
source: ERA5 hourly MSLP, 2021-2023 (Copernicus CDS)
normalization_method: percentile rank (lower raw = better); NaN imputed at 50

source: Satellite-derived annual-mean surface PM2.5 (WashU ACAG, van Donkelaar et al.), ~0.01° CONUS
source_date: ACAG V5 (most recent annual mean provided)

description: Chronic air quality: long-term annual-mean ground-level fine-particulate (PM2.5) concentration, sampled from a gridded satellite-derived surface. Lower PM2.5 scores higher. This is the standard air-pollution-epidemiology exposure metric and reflects persistent particulate burden (traffic, industry, agriculture, wood smoke, secondary aerosols) — a different geography from local wildfire burn hazard (score_wildfire), which the former episodic smoke-days metric largely duplicated. Full-grid coverage, so rural towns are not tied to a distant monitor.
source: Satellite-derived annual-mean surface PM2.5 (WashU ACAG, van Donkelaar et al.), ~0.01° CONUS
source_date: ACAG V5 (most recent annual mean provided)
normalization_method: percentile rank (lower raw = better); NaN imputed at 50

description: mean annual burn probability near town (unitless)
source: USDA FSim 270 m burn probability, CONUS (RDS-2016-0034-3)

description: Local wildfire hazard: mean modelled annual burn probability in the neighborhood of the town. Lower scores higher. Distinct from chronic particulate exposure (score_air_quality), which measures long-term PM2.5.
source: USDA FSim 270 m burn probability, CONUS (RDS-2016-0034-3)
normalization_method: percentile rank (lower raw = better); NaN imputed at 50

source: CDC reported Lyme cases by county (2023) / 2020 county population; CDC Ixodes established (context)
CDC reported Lyme cases by county (2023) / 2020 county population; CDC Ixodes established (context)

description: Lyme-vector tick (I. scapularis/pacificus) established in county (Y/N) — context only
source: CDC reported Lyme cases by county (2023) / 2020 county population; CDC Ixodes established (context)

description: Lyme disease risk as a weight (never a veto). County reported Lyme cases (2023) converted to an incidence rate (cases/100k) using county population. Lyme is strongly zero-inflated (most US counties report ≈none), so a county with 0 reported cases gets the top score (100, lowest risk) and only positive-incidence (Lyme-endemic) counties are inverse-rank-scored below that — higher incidence → lower score. Joined by county FIPS. Tick establishment is kept as a context raw, not scored.
source: CDC reported Lyme cases by county (2023) / 2020 county population; CDC Ixodes established (context)

description: distance to nearest large protected/wild area (km)
source: USGS PAD-US 4.0 (GAP 1/2, ≥100 km²) + NLCD land cover (CONUS, 30 m)
USGS PAD-US 4.0 (GAP 1/2, ≥100 km²) + NLCD land cover (CONUS, 30 m)

description: Access to wild nature. 0.5*percentile(close to nearest large GAP 1/2 protected area, ≥100 km²) + 0.5*percentile(high natural land-cover fraction — forest/shrub/grass/wetland — within 10 km). Higher = more natural surroundings and closer to protected wild land. Paired with isolation.
source: USGS PAD-US 4.0 (GAP 1/2, ≥100 km²) + NLCD land cover (CONUS, 30 m)
normalization_method: 0.5*pct(close to large protected area) + 0.5*pct(high natural cover)

description: great-circle distance to nearest large/medium hub airport (mi)
source: OurAirports — US large/medium airports with scheduled service
OurAirports — US large/medium airports with scheduled service

description: Air-travel access. Great-circle distance to the nearest US large- or medium-hub commercial airport (OurAirports, scheduled service). Closer scores higher.
source: OurAirports — US large/medium airports with scheduled service
normalization_method: percentile rank (lower raw = better); NaN imputed at 50

source: OpenStreetMap (Overpass) — Costco warehouses

description: Everyday amenity access, proxied by distance to the nearest Costco warehouse (OpenStreetMap). Closer scores higher. Low weight by design.
source: OpenStreetMap (Overpass) — Costco warehouses
normalization_method: percentile rank (lower raw = better); NaN imputed at 50

description: population within 50 km (sum of nearby places)
source: Census places + 2020 decennial population (derived; no new download)
Census places + 2020 decennial population (derived; no new download)
Census places + 2020 decennial population (derived; no new download)

description: Regional remoteness / isolation. 0.5*percentile(low population within 50 km) + 0.5*percentile(far from nearest city ≥ 50k). Higher = fewer people around. Population-within-radius captures true remoteness (a low-density suburb inside a metro is correctly not isolated); place density is kept only as a context raw. Paired with nature access.
source: Census places + 2020 decennial population (derived; no new download)

description: Democratic share of the two-party 2024 presidential vote in the town's precinct (%)
source: NYT 2024 presidential precinct results (national), spatial-joined to town centroid

description: Local political lean: Democratic share of the two-party presidential vote — Dem / (Dem + Rep) — in the precinct containing the town centroid (2024 general election), percentile-ranked so more Democratic-voting towns score higher. Precinct-level rather than county, so a left-leaning town inside a right-leaning county is captured. Measures how the area votes, not residents' ideology directly.
source: NYT 2024 presidential precinct results (national), spatial-joined to town centroid
normalization_method: percentile rank (higher raw = better); NaN imputed at 50
